channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Smash Bros. Dev Says It’s Impossible to Grant All Player Requests
item.content:encoded:
Masahiro Sakurai, the creator of Super Smash Bros and Kirby Air Riders, is now discussing the difficulties of trying to satisfy everyone while ensuring that their audience is heard and respected.

Nintendo recently released the fifth part in the Kirby Air Riders: Development Insights series. In the write-up, Sakurai speaks about the choices players have for self-expression in Air Riders. This includes different vehicles and headwear.

Sakurai noted that he wanted to cater to players who enjoy customizing their characters and thanked the team that worked on the game for allowing all characters to wear headgear.

Super Smash Bros. Creator: We Try to Keep Players in Mind

Furthermore, Masahiro Sakurai proudly stated that he always tries to keep players in mind when he’s developing a video game. However, plenty of time and labor can be added when accommodating player requests, and he knows there’s no way to appease everyone.

“I want to implement things because I understand the reasons behind the players’ desires. But it would be utterly impossible to grant every request. So we have to decide what should be implemented with that balance in mind. There will be players who think customization is pointless, but I definitely don’t feel like our efforts were in vain at all,” he said.

While this is in regard to Kirby Air Riders, much can be said in the same vein for the Super Smash Bros. series. There are plenty of player-requested characters that have yet to appear in the beloved Nintendo fighting game. While some third-party omissions, such as Devil May Cry’s Dante or Crash Bandicoot, make sense, many who love Nintendo are still waiting to play as Waluigi in Smash.

Super Smash Bros. Ultimate was released on the original Nintendo Switch in 2018. It would later have additional characters added via DLC packs. It is not known when or if another game in the series will be released.

What do you want to see from the next Smash Bros. game? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Jason Blundell Speaks On PlayStation Closing Dark Outlaw Games
item.content:encoded:
Jason Blundell comments on PlayStation’s closure of Dark Outlaw Games.

On March 24, PlayStation announced it was shutting down Dark Outlaw Games. This came just one year after the studio was formed by former Call of Duty Zombies lead Jason Blundell. Blundell is now commenting on the Studio’s closure, noting that there’s no ill will towards Sony.

Fans Would’ve Been Excited About Dark Outlaw Games’ Project, Says Blundell

Jason Blundell appeared on Twitch with streamer JCBackfire, who was also a designer at the studio, to discuss the disappointing turn of events. During the stream, he noted that focus changes all the time in this line of work, but he feels confident in the project they were developing, which they claim wasn’t a live-service title.

“I can reassure you—and it’s been reassured to me—it’s just times change, focus changes, but the project we were doing and what we were doing, fans would have been very excited,” Blundell said (via Kotaku).

Backfire also said he’d been working in game development for 5 years, and that all that time had been spent on things people would never get to play, saying they are “down but not out.”

Major Restructurings Are Dominating the Industry in 2026

Unfortunately, layoffs and restructurings continue to dominate the discussion in the video game space in the first quarter of 2026. Epic Games, known for producing Fortnite, recently laid off more than 1,000 people and announced that it was ending support for several game modes in Fortnite later this year.

Microsoft recently saw Phil Spencer retire, and Sarah Bond announced her resignation. The Xbox side of the gaming industry is currently working to rebuild its brand strength.

Nintendo is reportedly scaling back production of the Nintendo Switch 2 due to lower-than-expected demand, and several live-service games are shutting down faster than ever, such as Highguard.

How do you feel about all the shake-ups in gaming? What do you think is next for Jason Blundell? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

item.title: Pokémon Champions Will Have Balance Patches
item.content:encoded:
Pokémon Champions will release on Nintendo Switch and Nintendo Switch 2 on April 8 and will introduce various quality of life updates that will likely revolutionise the competitive Pokémon scene.

And in possibly the biggest of those changes, it has been confirmed that the free-to-play Pokémon battler will feature balance patches with updates to Pokémon. This could mean your favourite will one day have a chance of finally reaching Uber tier.

While speaking to Famitsu and other publications in a QnA, Pokémon Champions development director and game producer Masaaki Hoshino confirmed that balance changes are planned for the game:

“As this is a title that will be operated over the long term, we will make adjustments while closely monitoring player trends. We intend to always listen to the opinions of our players.”

It appears some balance changes have already been made to the game too, as discovered by those who attended early access events.

For starters, the move Protect has had its PP (number of uses) reduced from ten to eight, presumably to make games stall less often. Meanwhile, most moves have had their default PP increased with no way to increase a move’s PP in-game.

Additionally, popular VGC player Wolfe ‘Wolfey’ Glicke noticed that Gengar no longer has Encore, and Inceneroar can no longer use Knock Off.

Abilities for some Pokémon Legends Z-A Mega Evolutions have been confirmed too, including all-new abilities for Mega Meganium and Feraligatr. It has also been confirmed that Mega Dragonite will have Multiscale, while Mega Froslass will have Snow Warning.

Pokémon Champions Quality of Life Updates

Pokémon Champions is aiming to make battling and competitive play more accessible and less tedious, removing a lot of grinding that was previously needed to get the perfect stats on a Pokémon.

The main way it is accomplishing this in the removal of the IV system altogether. IVs have been a part of Pokémon for over two decades, giving Pokémon hidden boosts to stats decided by ‘genetics.’

In Pokémon Champions, mons will automatically have max IVs in everything, which is a rather polarising change as it removes certain strategies such as making an intentionally slower Pokémon for Trick Room.

Additionally, EVs, a similar increase to stats accomplished by battling Pokémon, have been replaced by a much simpler slider system with points to allocate to your mon’s stats.

Also, those who don’t have competitive-ready Pokémon to transfer from Pokémon Home will still be able to play Champions as there will be a built-in mode that allows people to acquire Pokémon from a randomised line-up using the non-purchasable Victory Points (VP) in-game currency.

Do you think Pokémon Champions is going to have a positive impact on competitive Pokémon? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And while it’s claimed the new game won’t be pay-to-win, Pokémon Champions will have a paid battle pass with prices now confirmed.

MOBILE APP
